semantics: These entries are applicability annotations for the tested build/target context. They do not replace the original version-based groundtruth labels.

CVE-2016-8617.reason: The vulnerable allocation/overflow condition is specific to 32-bit size_t builds. The tested curl binaries are ELF64 x86-64, so the vulnerable mechanism is not applicable even for versions that are version-labeled vulnerable or patched in the original groundtruth.
evidence: Metadata and patch hunk gate the added guard on SIZEOF_SIZE_T == 4.
The tested binaries are 64-bit x86-64 ELF targets.

CVE-2016-8625.reason: The vulnerable mechanism requires IDN support through the old libidn/IDNA2003 backend. The tested vulnerable-version binaries do not have IDN support compiled in, so the vulnerable backend is not applicable.
evidence: Local binary evidence shows IDN support is not present for these tested binaries.
No old libidn/stringprep/idna/tld backend imports were found in these tested binaries.
